topic.what: Metabolomics-based head-to-head comparison of electroacupuncture (EA) and moxibustion (Moxa) in a rat model of chronic atrophic gastritis (CAG), revealing distinct metabolic signatures despite comparable clinical improvement.
topic.how: CAG induced in Sprague-Dawley rats; EA and Moxa applied at ST36/CV12; serum and gastric tissue metabolomes profiled by UPLC-MS; multivariate statistics (PCA, PLS-DA) used to separate treatment signatures.
topic.why_matters: Demonstrates that two canonical TCM modalities applied at identical acupoints achieve similar outcomes via divergent metabolic routes — challenging the assumption of interchangeability and opening modality-specific mechanistic inquiry.

characteristics.what_it_measures: Degree to which EA and Moxa produce statistically distinguishable metabolome profiles in PLS-DA space
characteristics.value: Clear separation of EA and Moxa clusters from CAG model and from each other
characteristics.vs_baseline: CAG model group used as negative control; healthy rats as normative reference
characteristics.evidence: PLS-DA Q2 and permutation tests reported in study
characteristics.context: Animal model; metabolomic findings require human validation

characteristics.what_it_measures: Whether EA and Moxa produce statistically similar gastric mucosal improvement scores
characteristics.value: Comparable histological improvement between modalities despite divergent metabolomics
characteristics.evidence: Histopathological scoring of gastric tissue sections
characteristics.context: Endpoints limited to histology; functional measures (acid secretion, motility) not assessed

good_for: Researchers investigating modality-specific mechanisms of acupoint stimulation
Metabolomics-based TCM mechanistic studies requiring a comparative design template
Translational researchers seeking biomarker panels to distinguish EA from thermal stimulation effects
not_for: Direct clinical protocol selection without human trials
Diseases with different pathophysiology than CAG

novelty: First direct metabolomics comparison of EA vs. Moxa at same acupoints in the same disease model
Empirical demonstration that equivalent clinical outcomes can arise from distinct metabolic mechanisms — modality non-interchangeability
open_problems: Which specific metabolites are causally responsible vs. epiphenomenal to therapeutic effect
Whether the metabolic signatures translate to human CAG patients
Mechanisms linking thermal (Moxa) vs. electrical (EA) stimulation to divergent downstream metabolite profiles
